page: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is an x86_64 architecture cloud server within the Cloud GPU family. It features 16 Intel vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.25 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The server is designed for workloads requiring substantial graphics memory and parallel processing capabilities. The inclusion of the NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM makes it suitable for handling large-scale models and rendering tasks. Because the CPU allocation is shared, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU performance. The high memory-to-core ratio of 11.25 GB per core ensures that large datasets can be staged in system memory before being processed by the GPU.

This hardware configuration is optimized for artificial intelligence workloads, including large language model inference, machine learning training, and data science pipelines. It is also appropriate for high-fidelity graphics rendering, 3D modeling, and video transcoding tasks that leverage the Ada Lovelace architecture. The combination of 1200 GB of local NVMe storage and 180.0 GB of RAM supports data-intensive operations and local caching of large model weights.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is an x86_64 GPU-accelerated cloud server configured with 16 Intel vCPUs on a shared allocation model, 180.0 GB of system memory, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA L40S GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. This server delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 11.25 GB per core, making it highly efficient for staging large datasets. The shared CPU model offers a cost-effective alternative for GPU-intensive workloads that do not demand dedicated host CPU resources. It is optimized for machine learning inference, model training, data science pipelines, and high-fidelity graphics rendering.

tagline: An accelerator-focused cloud instance pairing an NVIDIA L40S GPU with high system memory for demanding AI and rendering workloads.
